retrieval_text: Apple is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 113 works and 149 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Apple. Common plant-part co-mentions include Fruit, Root, Berry, Leaf, Seed, Flower. Common preparation co-mentions include Tea, Juice, Decoction, Oil, Poultice, Powder. Common condition co-mentions include Fever, Cold or catarrh, Vomiting or nausea, Asthma, Wounds or ulcers, Rheumatism. Caution co-mentions include Child-use caution, Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Toxicity watch, Bleeding caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution. Representative source works include Herbal simples : approved for modern uses of cure / by W. T. Fernie.; Herbal simples : approved for modern uses of cure / by W.T. Fernie.; Herbal Simples Approved for Modern Uses of Cure; Dr. Mansperger's book of hygiene : containing instructions for the cure of diseases, together with many valuable and useful prescriptions : added to which is an accurate description of the properties and application of the curative powers of roots and herbs, with instructions for a diagnosis of diseases from the urine and pulse. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
